channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Police in Georgia's capital break up a tent camp set up by protesters demanding a new election
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Police in Georgia's capital early on Tuesday moved in to break up a tent camp that demonstrators set up on a central thoroughfare to protest the results of last month's parliamentary election and demand a new vote. The Oct.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: These are the best cruise lines for 2025
item.description: If you're looking to spend a vacation at sea but haven't decided which cruise line is best for your specific needs, a new online ranking shows the best options for every type of traveler, from families to luxury seekers. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Bear shot, killed by homeowner in Southern California
item.description: A California man is being criticized after he shot and killed a bear in his yard last week that he claimed was getting into his chicken coop.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, Fox 5

item.title: The Osprey's safety issues spiked over five years and caused deaths. Pilots still want to fly it
item.description: The Osprey is back in the air after being grounded for months following a crash last November that killed eight U.S. service members in Japan.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Kevin Vineys And Aaron Kessler, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Diddy tried to reach out to witnesses from jail, prosecutors allege
item.description: Combs has repeatedly "flouted rules" so he can "improperly impact the outcome of his case," prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: Kelley Connors, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Speaker Johnson's 'epic' weekend with Trump shows strengths and limits of his power
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — There was House Speaker Mike Johnson walking behind President-elect Donald Trump's entourage into Saturday night's UFC fight at Madison Square Garden, his stature overcome by the enormity of the scene around him. And Johnson mugging with musicians Kid Rock and Jelly Roll.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2 women tell Ethics panel Gaetz paid them for sex, attorney says
item.description: Two women testified before the House Ethics Committee that former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) paid them to have sex, according to the attorney representing them.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Georgia appeals court cancels hearing in election interference case against Trump
item.description: A Georgia appeals court has canceled oral arguments that were scheduled for next month on the appeal of a lower court ruling allowing Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis to continue to prosecute the election interference case she brought against President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Kate Brumback, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Father of Oxford shooting victim calls on community for help
item.description: Ethan Crumbley killed four students and injured several others in October of 2021 using a gun gifted by his father.
item.dc:creator: Caitlyn Becker, NewsNation

item.title: Bolton: Moscow, Beijing should feel ‘a lot of relief’ if Gabbard is confirmed as DNI
item.description: Former national security adviser John Bolton said that Russia and China should feel “a lot of relief” if former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii) is confirmed as President-elect Trump’s choice for director of national intelligence. “She’s not capable of doing it,” Bolton said Sunday on CNN.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Pennsylvania's high court orders counties not to count disputed ballots in US Senate race
item.description: Pennsylvania’s state Supreme Court is weighing in on a flashpoint amid ongoing vote counting in the U.S. Senate election between Democratic Sen. Bob Casey and Republican David McCormick.
item.dc:creator: Marc Levy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Police seek witnesses, cell phone video after gunfire during a parade in New Orleans kills 2
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Deadly shootings at a signature New Orleans cultural event — a neighborhood “second-line” parade — left authorities pleading with witnesses to come forward with information or cell phone video while seeking to reassure the public that the weekend bloodshed was an aberration in a c
item.dc:creator: KEVIN McGILL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 1 in 5 get news from social media influencers: Pew poll
item.description: Of the 21 percent of adults who say they get their news from influencers on social media, the split is roughly even across parties.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, NewsNation

item.title: 2 are killed and 1 is critically wounded in New York City knife rampage. A suspect is in custody
item.description: Officials say a man has carried out a string of stabbings across a swath of Manhattan that killed two people and critically wounded a third. The 51-year-old suspect is in police custody after Monday morning's attacks.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How a 1966 wedding vinyl ended up at a Texas thrift store — and found its way back home
item.description: A 1966 wedding record is finally making its way back home after accidentally being donated to an Austin, Texas, thrift store.
item.dc:creator: Kelsey Thompson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Diddy tried to reach out to witnesses from jail, prosecutors allege
item.description: Combs has repeatedly "flouted rules" so he can "improperly impact the outcome of his case," prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: Kelley Connors, NewsNation

item.title: 1 in 5 get news from social media influencers: Pew poll
item.description: Sixty-five percent of those who get news from social media say influencers have bettered their understanding of current events and civic issues.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, Fox 5

item.title: Southwest employee welcomes Chiefs fans by playing Bills song over intercom
item.description: She said she wanted to let Chiefs fans know that "the Buffalo Bills have the best fans in the league.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Minkewicz, NewsNation

item.title: Former officer sentenced for beating a man with a baton 45 times in Los Angeles
item.description: A former police officer with the Veterans Affairs Police Department (VAPD) has been sentenced for hitting an unarmed man dozens of times with a baton, officials announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Daniel Penny trial resumes with third day of testimony from medical examiner, questions over cause of death
item.description: Dr. Cynthia Harris returned to the witness stand for her third day of testimony Monday in the trial against Daniel Penny for chokehold that killed Jordan Neely.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trial begins for Florida man in human smuggling case after freezing deaths of Indian family at Canada-US border
item.description: FERGUS FALLS, Minn. (AP) — Federal prosecutors plan to argue at a Minnesota trial that two men were part of a criminal network stretching from India to Canada that smuggled families seeking better lives in the United States.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL GOLDBERG Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How to stop using the same password over and over
item.description: The most common password is 123456. How long does it take to crack that? Less than one second. That's the findings from NordPass, which has been studying the most common passwords for six years. It's time to start using a password manager. Here's how.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Beyoncé performing at halftime of NFL Christmas Day game on Netflix
item.description: Beyoncé is coming to your home on Christmas — provided you have Netflix and are tuning in to the Baltimore Ravens-Houston Texans game.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden authorizes Ukraine to use US-supplied long-range missiles for deeper strikes inside Russia
item.description: President Joe Biden has authorized the use of U.S.-supplied long-range missiles by Ukraine to strike even deeper inside Russia, the latest easing of limitations meant to prevent the conflict from further spiraling, according to one U.S. official and three people familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cruise line offers 4-year journey to 'skip forward' in wake of US presidential election
item.description: While the cruise line doesn't explicitly say the offer was brought on by the election, the two-year option was dubbed the 'Mid-Term Selection.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WGN 9

item.title: Need credit card debt relief? Debt management could help
item.description: If you can’t get favorable terms through a hardship plan or your issuer doesn’t offer this assistance, there’s still an option to consider.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Lambarena, Fox 5

item.title: Biden becomes the first sitting US president to visit the Amazon rainforest
item.description: Joe Biden on Sunday became the first sitting American president to set foot in the Amazon rainforest, as the incoming Trump administration seems poised to scale back the U.S. commitment to combating climate change.
item.dc:creator: KRON4

item.title: Trump makes brief remark about running again in 2028
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump, during a meeting with House Republicans on Wednesday, made an apparent joke in which he remarked that the GOP might want to “do something” that would somehow allow him to serve a third term in 2028.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: US students who box, skydive and help youth and asylum seekers are among 2025's Rhodes scholars
item.description: A group of 32 students from the United States have been selected to attend the University of Oxford as part of the prestigious Rhodes scholar program in the coming year.
item.dc:creator: Brian P. D. Hannon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Man arrested for allegedly trying to smuggle hundreds of tarantulas and centipedes on flight
item.description: A South Korean man was arrested in Peru for allegedly attempting to smuggle hundreds of tarantulas and centipedes on a flight back to Korea.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: From 'The Exorcist' to 'Heretic,' why holy horror can be a hit with moviegoers
item.description: In the new horror movie, “Heretic,” Hugh Grant plays a diabolical religious skeptic who traps two scared missionaries in his house and tries to violently shake their faith.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY MEYER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: FBI investigates offensive messages targeting Hispanic, LGBTQ people
item.description: The announcement comes days after African American students received similar messages stating they’d been selected to "pick cotton.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: No. 13 Purdue rediscovers strong post presence in 87-78 win over No. 2 Alabama
item.description: Boilermakers forward Trey Kaufman-Renn made 12 of 23 shots, scored a career-high 26 points and grabbed eight rebounds as No. 13 Purdue handed No. 2 Alabama an 87-78 defeat that was eerily reminiscent of last season's 92-86 loss to the Boilermakers in Toronto.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL MAROT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Thousands of Netflix users report outages during Tyson-Paul boxing match
item.description: Just as thousands of Netflix users flocked to the app to stream Friday night's head-to-head boxing match between champion Mike Tyson and YouTuber-turned-boxer Jake Paul, the stream crashed.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nuggets star Nikola Jokic and coach Michael Malone miss game against Pelicans for personal reasons
item.description: The Denver Nuggets were without star center Nikola Jokic and coach Michael Malone for their NBA Cup opener Friday night against the New Orleans Pelicans.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Southwest flight evacuated after cell phone catches fire
item.description: Passengers of a Southwest Airlines flight sitting at a Denver International Airport gate had to evacuate after a cell phone battery caught on fire.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, WFLA, NBC

item.title: California man sentenced to life for hate crime in the killing of a gay college student
item.description: SANTA ANA, Calif. (AP) — A California man convicted of stabbing to death a gay University of Pennsylvania student in an act of hate was sentenced Friday to life without parole in prison.
item.dc:creator: AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, ABC 4
